description: Network-wide role and position catalogue covering Eduversal HQ (CH), partner-school leadership (AH), teaching staff (TH), HQ-side subject specialists, induction and student-support functions. Source: 'Career_Growth_Program.xlsx Roles sheet (40 rows)' + Phase-2 HR/educator review (2026-05-13). Gap-flagged where the source omitted required HR fields; advisory items marked 'gap_added' are HR/educator additions not present in the original sheet.
scope: SMP (Grade 7-9) + SMA (Grade 10-12). SD (Grade 1-6) coverage is deferred; TK/Early Years out of scope. K-12 expansion requires a scope review.
purposeForEduversal: Single English-language reference catalogue cross-referencing each position with the role architecture in CLAUDE.md (per-platform role fields + sub-roles + ch_subjects[]), the three rating systems (KPI / Appraisal v2.1 / Competency framework), Cambridge Teacher / School Leader Standards 2023, and Indonesian regulatory roles (Dapodik / BOS / UU PDP / DSL). Drives the CH /roles-positions diagram page.

advisoryDisclaimers.title: Reports-to topology — Eduversal Framework v1.0 (2026)
advisoryDisclaimers.body: The reportsTo[] arrays and dual-line org chart are canonically derived from the School Organizational & Meeting Framework v1.0 (Eduversal Foundation, 2026) — see docs/research/eduversal/organizational-meeting-framework-v1-2026.json. Four non-negotiable rules from §2.1: (NN1) Principals and Guidance Counselors report at the same level to FR/GM; (NN2) divisional symmetry (KG/SD/SMP/SMA replicate identical templates); (NN3) strict reporting hierarchy; (NN4) Support Services (Marketing/Finance/Facility/Alumni) report directly to FR/GM. Framework is Partner-School-Edition scoped; Eduversal HQ internal topology pending separate canonical reference.

advisoryDisclaimers.title: MBTI is reference-only — not a hiring or promotion criterion
advisoryDisclaimers.body: MBTI test-retest reliability is well-documented as low (≈50% category drift over weeks). The 'mbtiSuggested' field is preserved for cultural continuity with the source spreadsheet, but it MUST NOT be used as a screening, hiring, promotion or termination signal. Use competency-based behavioural indicators (see competencyTrack + cambridgeRefs fields) for HR decisions. Big Five (OCEAN) is recommended for any future personality-style instrument.

advisoryDisclaimers.title: Teaching-Hours Equivalent values inherit source-sheet ambiguity
advisoryDisclaimers.body: Values range from 4 (Lab / Librarian) to 21 (Nurse / IT) in the source sheet without published rationale. They are preserved verbatim with field 'teachingHoursEquivalent.source = source-sheet'. A formal methodology key (formula + base load + role-weighting) MUST be published before this column is used for salary, contract-load or workload-fairness decisions. Superseded for the 24 checked rows in the Equivalent.pdf source by the 'teachingHoursModel' triplet (optimum / equivalent / suggested) — see next disclaimer.

advisoryDisclaimers.title: Teaching-Hours Model (optimum / equivalent / suggested) — added 2026-05-14
advisoryDisclaimers.body: Equivalent.pdf (Career Growth Equivalent Hours sheet) carries a three-column breakdown for 24 checked positions: Optimum Teaching Hours (24 baseline contact load per week, fixed for every teaching role), Equivalent Teaching Hours (hours deducted as an allowance for the non-teaching responsibilities the role carries — e.g. Principal -18, Subject Leader -4), and Suggested Teaching Hours (the remainder Optimum - Equivalent — what the role is expected to actually teach). Stored on each position as `teachingHoursModel: { optimum, equivalent, suggested }`. Non-teaching / specialist roles (Public Relations, Designer, IT, Nurse, etc.) carry `-` in the source for all three columns and are stored as `null`. The 'Community Engagement 1/2' rows use a different base (20 not 24) and are tagged accordingly.

advisoryDisclaimers.title: Certification Tier (Certificated / Classified / AM&C) — added 2026-05-14
advisoryDisclaimers.body: Equivalent.pdf assigns each filled row to one of three Indonesian-staffing tiers: 'Certificated' (sertifikasi guru pathway — formal teaching credential required), 'Classified' (classified staff — non-instructional support roles with formal grading), 'Administrative, Management & Confidential (AM&C)' (admin / leadership / confidential roles, typically non-graded contracts). Stored on each position as `certificationTier`. Empty in source = unspecified, recorded as `null`. This is a payroll / contracting signal, not an HR competency claim — refer to Eduversal Indonesia HR for the operating definitions of each tier.

advisoryDisclaimers.title: Indonesian regulatory roles are flagged but not exhaustively specified
advisoryDisclaimers.body: Dapodik operator, BOS fund manager, DSL (Designated Safeguarding Lead), DPO (UU PDP 2024) and SEN/EAL coordinator roles are included as gap-fill items. Their statutory citations are placeholders pending Eduversal legal/HR review. Do not treat this document as legal compliance evidence.

leaders.label: Cambridge School Leader Standards 2023 (25 standards × 4 levels)

positions.title: Principal (General)
positions.scopeLabel: Network role definition — applies when SMP/SMA distinction is not relevant
positions.description: The Principal is the head of the school and is responsible for overseeing all academic, administrative, and extracurricular activities.
majorDuties: Develops and implements the school's vision, mission, and goals
Hires, trains, and evaluates staff
Oversees academic programs and curriculum
Ensures compliance with educational regulations and standards
Represents the school to the community and parents
otherDuties: Coordinates with external agencies and community organizations
Manages crisis situations and maintains school safety
minimumQualifications: Master's degree in Education, Educational Leadership, or related field
5+ years of experience in educational leadership roles (e.g., Vice Principal or Principal)
Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills
Knowledge of educational policies and regulations
Experience in budgeting and resource management
positions.mbtiNote: Source omitted. See advisoryDisclaimers.mbti — do not use MBTI as a hiring filter.
trainingDevelopment: Leadership and Management Training (strategic planning, decision-making, conflict resolution, team building)
Instructional Leadership (improving teaching practices, curriculum development, performance assessment)
Professional Development Workshops on current educational trends and policies
positions.deprecation_note: Replaced by per-division principal/guidance roles per Framework v1.0 (2026). Kept for back-compat; not used in active rendering.

positions.title: Principal SMA (High School Principal)
positions.description: The High School Principal is the academic and administrative leader responsible for overseeing the high school division (Grades 10-12). They ensure the implementation of the school's mission, curriculum, policies, and student welfare while fostering a positive learning environment.
majorDuties: Lead, supervise, and evaluate high school faculty and staff
Develop and implement academic programs aligned with international standards (IB, AP, IGCSE, A-Level)
Monitor student progress, discipline, and well-being
Collaborate with the school board and parents
Ensure compliance with accreditation standards and educational regulations
Oversee budget planning and resource allocation for the high school division
otherDuties: Represent the school at conferences and community events
Handle crisis management and conflict resolution
minimumQualifications: Master's degree in Education Leadership or related field
Minimum 8-10 years of teaching and administrative experience in an international school
Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills
Familiarity with international curricula (IB, AP, A-Level, IGCSE)
positions.miNote: Source incomplete. Recommend Intrapersonal + Interpersonal + Logical-Mathematical mirroring the general Principal profile.
positions.trainingNote: Source incomplete. Mirror general Principal training catalogue + Cambridge School Leader CPD pathways.

positions.title: Principal SMP (Middle School Principal)
positions.scopeLabel: Grades 7-9 (Indonesian SMP standard — confirms source inconsistency)
positions.description: The Middle School Principal oversees Grades 7-9, ensuring a smooth transition between elementary and high school while fostering academic and social-emotional growth. This is the lead leadership role at the Eduversal Y7-8 pilot tier (2026-05-10).
positions.gapFlag: Source row was incomplete — major duties, qualifications and developmental fields below are reconstructed by scaling Principal SMA scope down to Y7-9. Validation by HR pending.
majorDuties: Lead, supervise and evaluate middle-school faculty and staff
Implement Cambridge Lower Secondary + Indonesian Kurikulum Merdeka curriculum across Y7-9
Coordinate transition pathways: Y6 intake from feeder schools + Y9-to-Y10 articulation to high school
Champion social-emotional learning (SEL) appropriate to early adolescence
Monitor pastoral care and OSIS programs
Manage middle-school budget and resource allocation
otherDuties: Coordinate Cambridge Lower Secondary Checkpoint preparation
Lead parent communication for grade-level transitions
minimumQualifications: Master's degree in Education Leadership or related field
Minimum 8-10 years of teaching and administrative experience including SMP-age students
Familiarity with Cambridge Lower Secondary + Indonesian SMP standards
Experience in adolescent development and SEL
positions.mbtiNote: Inferred parallel to SMA Principal; preserves source-sheet sensibility.

positions.title: Vice Principal (General)
positions.description: The Vice Principal assists the Principal in managing the school, focusing on specific operational, disciplinary, and student-related responsibilities. Stands in for the Principal when absent — full crisis authority during that window MUST be documented in a separate Authority Matrix (see gap_added position 'authority_matrix' notes).
majorDuties: Oversees specific academic or administrative areas (e.g. curriculum, discipline, assessment)
Coordinates staff development and training
Represents the Principal in their absence
otherDuties: Coordinate student services and parent engagement
Act as liaison between students, teachers, and parents
Take charge of school activities in the absence of the Principal
minimumQualifications: Master's degree in Education or related field
3+ years of experience in a school leadership role (e.g., Department Head or Coordinator)
Ability to handle student-related issues effectively

positions.title: Vice Principal SMA
positions.description: Supports the High School Principal in administrative, academic, and disciplinary functions, ensuring smooth daily operations.
majorDuties: Assist in teacher evaluations and scheduling
Lead disciplinary and attendance interventions
Supervise standardized testing and academic reporting
Stand in for the Principal when necessary
minimumQualifications: 5+ years of teaching at SMA level

positions.title: Vice Principal SMP
positions.description: Works alongside the Middle School Principal to ensure effective operations, student discipline, and teacher support.
positions.gapFlag: Source row near-empty. Scaled from Vice Principal SMA + SMP context.
majorDuties: Assist in teacher evaluations and scheduling for Y7-9 cohort
Lead disciplinary, attendance and pastoral interventions
Coordinate Cambridge Lower Secondary Checkpoint operations
Stand in for the Principal SMP when necessary
otherDuties: Liaise with SD feeder schools for Y6 intake
Coordinate cross-grade activities with Vice Principal SMA
minimumQualifications: 5+ years of teaching at SMP level

positions.title: Subject Leader
positions.description: The Subject Leader takes on the overall management of a subject area within the school — curriculum, assessment, teacher mentorship, resource planning. Within Eduversal they also own subject-pacing approval, chapter-test review participation, and AFT mentor assignment for their subject.
majorDuties: Curriculum Development — design and implement subject curriculum aligned with Cambridge + KM standards
Instructional Support — coach teachers within the subject area; share resources and best practices
Assessment Coordination — set benchmarks, analyse student performance data, inform teaching adjustments
Professional Development — organize subject-specific training and workshops
Collaboration — foster a subject-team culture of shared planning and peer observation
Quality Assurance — monitor teaching quality through classroom observations + walkthroughs
Resource Management — manage subject budget, textbooks, lab/digital tools
Communication — liaise with parents and school administration
Strategic Planning — contribute to school strategy regarding the subject area
Student Support — advocate for inclusive curriculum access
otherDuties: Lead the weekly subject meeting and minute it
Collaborate with subject teachers on student outcomes (academic + non-academic)
Allocate teaching hours across the subject team
Plan weekly lessons / P5 / PBL units
Coordinate with homeroom teachers for grade-level chair programme
Provide mentors for AFT and PPL participants in the subject
(Eduversal-specific) Participate in chapter-test review + EASE item ownership for the subject
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree (S1) in subject specialty + teaching certification

positions.title: AFT Coordinator
positions.description: The AFT (Apprenticeship for Teaching) Coordinator is responsible for the overall coordination of pre-service teacher placements at the school site. They orchestrate interactions among mentor teachers, pre-service teachers and the site, maximising programme effectiveness.
majorDuties: Recommend Mentor teachers competent to supervise pre-service teachers
Provide site orientation to pre-service teachers (on-site hours, rules, behaviour management policies, assessment requirements)
Meet weekly to support pre-service teacher development; arrange cross-class observation visits and provide feedback
Encourage pre-service teachers to become part of the wider education community
Evaluate pre-service teacher performance through direct observation or via Mentor teacher discussions
Provide feedback on progress and performance
Be available to discuss professional challenges
otherDuties: Assist mentors and mentees with the overall programme
Communicate with the AFT Coordinator Centre (network HQ)
Lead regular meetings with mentors + mentees
Communicate guidance programmes for mentees with school counsellors
Promote the AFT programme to teacher-training institutions
positions.teachingHoursNote: Source-sheet value. Equivalent.pdf supersedes with 24/4/20 triplet for the 'AFT Coordinator' checked row.

positions.title: OSIS Coordinator
positions.scopeLabel: Indonesia-specific. Conditionally applicable — only at schools that operate the Indonesian Kurikulum Merdeka pastoral programme.
positions.description: The OSIS (Organisasi Siswa Intra Sekolah) Coordinator manages student-affairs programmes, leads OSIS meetings, and sets policy for school-wide student-led programmes. Indonesia-mandated under KM curriculum but optional in Cambridge-only schools.
positions.applicabilityCondition: School runs KM curriculum and/or has registered an OSIS chapter with the Ministry.
majorDuties: Leadership — guide and oversee OSIS members in planning + execution
Planning Events — cultural festivals, sports competitions, community service
Communication — liaise between students and school administration
Collaboration — align OSIS activities with educational goals
Training and Development — facilitate leadership training for OSIS members
otherDuties: Create OSIS vision, mission and yearly goals
Report annual programmes to counsellors and principal
trainingDevelopment: Networking with OSIS coordinators at peer schools

positions.title: Project Coordinator
positions.description: Manages project competitions and seminars, supervises project groups and experiments, selects supervisors, and produces annual information on official competitions.
majorDuties: Project Planning — develop project plans, timelines, budgets
Communication — serve as primary point of contact between project stakeholders
Task Management — coordinate and monitor project tasks
Resource Allocation — manage personnel, materials, equipment
Documentation — maintain accurate project documentation
Risk Management — identify potential risks and plan mitigation
Budget Tracking — monitor expenses + adherence to budget
Quality Control — ensure project deliverables meet quality standards
otherDuties: Provide consultation sessions with students + project supervisor teachers
Coordinate with PR / media for achievement coverage

positions.title: Teacher (Subject Teacher)
positions.description: An educator responsible for facilitating learning, developing students' academic and social skills, and fostering a positive classroom environment. Within Eduversal: maps to TH `subject_teacher` sub-role; subject of KPI, Appraisal v2.1 and the Teaching Competency Framework (CTS 2023).
majorDuties: Instruction — deliver lessons adapting methods to learning styles
Assessment — evaluate student performance and provide constructive feedback
Curriculum Development — design and implement lesson plans aligned with educational standards
Classroom Management — create a safe, respectful, focused classroom
Support — provide guidance to students addressing individual needs
Communication — maintain open lines with students, parents, colleagues
Professional Development — engage in ongoing training and CPD
Mentorship — serve as role model, encouraging critical thinking and personal growth
otherDuties: Serve as Head of Department, mentor, supervisor, or homeroom teacher
Collaborate with community for service projects
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree (S1) in Education or non-Education subject
Teaching certificate (Indonesia: Akta Mengajar / Sertifikasi Guru)
positions.teachingHoursNote: Source-sheet value; serves as the base load.

positions.title: Homeroom Teacher
positions.description: A subject teacher with additional pastoral responsibility for one form class — coordinating character education delivery, parent communication, discipline agenda and classroom logistics. Eduversal Note: teaching-hours equivalent is not formally reduced in the source sheet; HR recommends a 4-hour reduction (16-18 contact hours) to accommodate pastoral load.
majorDuties: Build communication with parents; report regularly on student development
Distribute student report cards and explain them to parents
Surface feedback from parents and students to Guidance Coordinator + Principal
Manage class discipline agenda
Set class structure — seating, daily duties roster, class rules, class leader
Maintain classroom inventory
otherDuties: Conduct 1-on-1 student meetings + group discussions + class activities
positions.teachingHoursNote: Source omitted in original sheet. Equivalent.pdf (Homeroom Teacher SMP-SMA row) now provides 24/8/16 triplet. The 8-hour allowance accommodates the pastoral duty — closely matches HR's earlier recommendation of '~4 hours below base teacher'.

positions.title: Guidance Coordinator
positions.description: Owns guidance activities involving students, dormitory advisors, teachers, staff, alumni and parents. Reports to school management (GM / Principal) and evaluates the guidance programme with the management board.
majorDuties: Plan guidance programme (with Parent Coordinator, Homeroom Teachers, Alumni, College Counsellor)
Design school + dormitory character-education programme (values-based, seminars, school culture)
Follow up on student conditions (motivation, problems, career planning)
Plan In-House Training for Homeroom Teachers on student-guidance topics
Coordinate with Parent Coordinator on parent-impact programmes
Follow up on dormitory activities
Design dormitory-advisor development programmes
Map student potential + interests with College Counsellor
Follow up on alumni engagement with Alumni Coordinator
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree in Psychology or School Counselling
positions.deprecation_note: Replaced by per-division principal/guidance roles per Framework v1.0 (2026). Kept for back-compat; not used in active rendering.

positions.title: College Counsellor
positions.scopeLabel: Primarily SMA (Y10-12). At SMP level pivots to academic exploration / talent discovery rather than university preparation.
positions.description: Owns students' career-planning programme. At SMA, focuses on university selection + admissions; at SMP, pivots to talent discovery and academic exploration.
majorDuties: Monthly seminars on agreed network-wide themes
Annual education exhibition with universities worldwide
Regular student meetings on university preferences
Career-planning syllabus across Y7-12 (Y7-9 = talent discovery; Y10-12 = university prep + UTBK)
≥ 2 university visits per semester (SMA cohort)
Portfolio + skills building for overseas + UTBK pathways
Parent communication on university planning
minimumQualifications: Background in school counselling, psychology, or social work
English language proficiency (for international university applications)

positions.title: Parent Coordinator
positions.description: Liaison between school administration and parents/guardians. Facilitates communication, promotes parental involvement, and supports families through the school system. Boundary vs Public Relations (Internal): Parent Coordinator does case-by-case parent care; PR (Internal) does broadcast communications.
majorDuties: Primary contact between parents + school on policies, events, student progress
Organise + promote parent workshops + meetings + events
Offer support + resources (counselling pointers, tutoring, community programmes)
otherDuties: Run parent training (supporting student learning at home)
Coordinate parent-teacher conferences, open houses, community engagement

positions.title: Alumni Coordinator
positions.description: Develops + maintains the alumni network (events, newsletters, activities). Covers two cohorts: studying alumni (university) + working alumni (career-track). Typical contract: 3 days/week teaching + 3 days/week alumni programmes (HR clarification needed on full-time/part-time status — see source-issues).
positions.gapFlag: Source claims 3+3 days/week (= 6 work days). HR must clarify full-time vs part-time status + corresponding salary scale.
majorDuties: Update alumni database (university attended, workplace)
Communicate with alumni
Visit alumni
Organise alumni events
Attend + organise meetings
minimumQualifications: Good communication, organizational, and data-analysis skills

positions.title: Dormitory Admin
positions.scopeLabel: Schools with residential / boarding facilities only.
positions.description: Responsible for daily operations of student dormitories: resident concerns, housing policy compliance, events, safety, cleanliness, and student-school liaison. Manages budgets, meetings, conflicts. Creates an inclusive residential community.
majorDuties: Recognize resident needs + concerns; facilitate conflict resolution
Provide resident guidance + support
Ensure compliance with housing policies + safety regulations
Manage budgets + records + administrative paperwork
Oversee dormitory safety/security; conduct inspections + emergency drills
Act as liaison between residents and school
otherDuties: Respond to resident emergencies / crises; coordinate with school services
Supervise resident assistants + dorm advisors; train + support them
Create a positive, inclusive living environment
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree (Education, Social Work, Management, or related)
Prior experience in residence life / student affairs (often as a resident assistant)
positions.hierarchyNote: Source omitted upward boarding hierarchy. Larger boarding operations would interpose 'Boarding Director' between Dormitory Admin and Principal; per-floor 'Resident Master/Mistress' beneath Dorm Admin. Gap-flagged.

positions.title: School Nurse
positions.description: Promotes and maintains the health and well-being of students, teachers, and staff. Provides medical care, health education and support services for a safe, healthy learning environment.
majorDuties: Health assessment and screening
Healthcare management
Health education and promotion
Health record management
Health counselling and reporting
Emergency preparedness and response
minimumQualifications: Nursing or medical degree (Indonesia: STR aktif)
Excellent interpersonal skills with tact, patience, courtesy
Modern office practices (filing, statistical record-keeping, basic Office software)
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet value 21 — queried by HR. Equivalent.pdf reclassifies School Nurse as non-teaching (no Optimum/Equivalent/Suggested triplet) and tags as Certificated tier.

positions.title: School Psychologist
positions.description: Supports students' mental health, learning, and development in educational settings. Assesses needs, provides counselling, and collaborates with educators and parents.
majorDuties: Conduct psychological assessments and evaluations
Develop and implement interventions for learning/behaviour issues
Provide counselling for social, emotional, academic challenges
Collaborate with teachers to create supportive learning environments
Consult with parents on student progress and needs
Develop Individualized Education Plans (IEPs) for students with disabilities
otherDuties: Conduct workshops + training for staff + parents
Stay updated on research and best practices
minimumQualifications: Master's or Doctoral degree in School Psychology / Clinical Psychology
trainingDevelopment: Continuing education in mental health + educational strategies
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet single-column value 18. Equivalent.pdf classifies School Psychologist as non-teaching (no triplet) and tags as Certificated tier.
positions.boundaryNote: vs Guidance Coordinator: Psychologist conducts clinical assessment + intervention; Guidance Coordinator owns programme strategy + non-clinical pastoral coordination. Report-line: Psychologist reports to Guidance Coordinator.

positions.title: Front Office Staff
positions.description: First point of contact for visitors, students, parents and external parties. Creates a welcoming and organized environment while supporting administrative operations.
majorDuties: Reception + visitor management — greet, log, issue passes, answer calls
Communication support — handle email/phone inquiries, distribute notices, support newsletters
Student services — assist late/early-leave students, maintain attendance records, first-line support for unwell students before nurse/counsellor referral
Administrative assistance — filing, photocopying, data entry, supplies + inventory
Record keeping + documentation — visitor logs, mail, school forms
otherDuties: Event coordination support (parent meetings, graduation, open houses)
minimumQualifications: High school diploma or equivalent; office training preferred
positions.mbtiNote: Source omitted. Inferred from interpersonal-heavy reception role.

positions.title: Secretary / School Admin
positions.description: Manages overall administrative functions of the school: student records, staff/HR admin, financial admin, secretarial + Dapodik operator duties.
majorDuties: Student Administration — records, grades, attendance, transfers, registration
Staff and HR Administration — staff records, attendance, leave, internal documents
Financial Administration — BOS funds, financial reports, receipts/invoices
Secretarial Tasks — correspondence, schedules, archive
General Office Support — info services, event support
otherDuties: Serve as operator for Dapodik (Indonesian education data system)
positions.certificationTierNote: Equivalent.pdf carries two adjacent rows: 'School Admin' (tier unspecified) and 'Secretary' (AM&C). This catalogue treats them as the same role; the AM&C tier from the Secretary row is preferred because the Dapodik / BOS / confidential-correspondence duties dominate the workload.

positions.title: Legal Officer
positions.description: Manages legal affairs of the institution. Ensures the school operates within the legal framework and maintains a safe, compliant environment.
positions.gapFlag: Source profile near-empty (only description + duties). Fields below from HR fill.
majorDuties: Provide legal advice on contracts, education law compliance, dispute resolution
Draft and review legal documents (policies, contracts, agreements)
Ensure compliance with all relevant laws and regulations
Identify potential legal risks and advise on mitigation
Represent the school in legal proceedings
otherDuties: Liaise with external counsel on complex matters
Maintain regulatory-change watch (UU PDP, Permendikbud updates)
minimumQualifications: 3+ years legal practice or in-house counsel experience

positions.title: Accountant
positions.description: Records, analyses, and interprets financial information. Tracks institutional financial activities and ensures accuracy + compliance with applicable laws.
majorDuties: Prepare and examine financial records
Manage budgets and financial plans
Ensure taxes are calculated and paid correctly
Audit financial statements for accuracy and fraud
Provide advice on financial decisions and strategies
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
2+ years of experience in accounting or financial management
Strong attention to detail + analytical skills
positions.miNote: Source listed competencies (Accounting + Financial Knowledge, Analytical Thinking, Integrity, Communication, Time Management, Teamwork) here instead of MI types. HR replaces with standard MI mapping.

positions.title: Public Relations (Internal)
positions.description: Builds and maintains positive communication and relationships within the school community — teachers, staff, students, parents, school board. Owns internal-channel communication consistency. Boundary vs Parent Coordinator: PR (Internal) does broadcast / many-to-one; Parent Coordinator does case-by-case / one-to-one.
majorDuties: Develop + implement internal communication strategies
Prepare + distribute newsletters, announcements, updates
Maintain internal channels (bulletins, email, intranet, WhatsApp groups)
Foster positive school culture via internal events (assemblies, appreciation days, open houses)
Act as liaison between school leadership and stakeholders
Gather + report internal feedback for continuous improvement
otherDuties: Draft internal communications during emergencies / sensitive situations
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree in Communications / PR / Education Management
Digital comms + basic design tools (Canva, MS Publisher)
positions.certificationTierNote: Equivalent.pdf lists 'Public Relations' once (no tier) under the Pupil-support category. This catalogue splits the role into Internal / External — neither row carries an explicit tier in the source.

positions.title: Public Relations (External)
positions.description: Manages + enhances the school's image and reputation to the public, media, prospective parents, alumni, government agencies, and the wider community. Focuses on strategic relationships and consistent positive external communication.
majorDuties: Maintain school brand identity (logo, colours, tone of voice)
Craft press releases, articles, features
Ensure positive media coverage + respond to media inquiries
Manage official communication with external stakeholders (regulators, alumni, sponsors, partners)
Maintain school website + official social media
Prepare brochures, prospectuses, marketing materials

positions.title: Digital Marketing Specialist
positions.description: Enhances the school's online presence and attracts new students. Creative + strategic individual using digital channels to drive enrolment.
majorDuties: Develop + execute digital marketing strategies
Manage social media channels
Create + optimize website content
Run email marketing campaigns
Manage paid advertising
Analytics + reporting
trainingDevelopment: Workshops + conferences + online courses on digital marketing trends
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet single-column value 21 — queried by HR. Equivalent.pdf reclassifies as non-teaching specialist (no triplet, no tier).

positions.title: Graphic Designer
positions.description: Creates visually engaging designs for school materials. Translates school vision into compelling graphics, maintains branding consistency, enhances school image through high-quality visual content.
majorDuties: Project Management
Content Creation
Graphic Design (layout, typography, illustration, branding)
Quality Control
minimumQualifications: Graphic design degree helpful but not essential
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet single-column value 21. Equivalent.pdf reclassifies as non-teaching specialist (no triplet, no tier).

positions.title: Content Creator
positions.description: Produces creative written, visual, and multimedia content for the school's communication and marketing channels.
positions.gapFlag: Source row contained only the role title + a supporting-skills note. HR fills below from skill notes + adjacent role data.
majorDuties: Produce written, visual + multimedia content for school comms channels
Functions as copy writer, videographer, photographer, video editor as needed
Maintain editorial calendar in coordination with PR (External) + Digital Marketing
otherDuties: Support PR (External) on campaign content production
minimumQualifications: Skilled in copywriting, video editing (Adobe Premiere or DaVinci Resolve), photography

positions.title: IT Staff
positions.description: Provides technical support to teachers, staff, students. Maintains hardware/software, AV equipment, network admin, software support, cybersecurity. Merged from source rows 16 + 37 (IT Admin and IT Staff treated as same role per editor's note).
majorDuties: Manage + maintain technology infrastructure + systems
IT Support / Help Desk
System Administration
Network Administration
Cybersecurity
Manage SIS, databases, software for academic + admin
Install + maintain hardware + software
Ensure network security + data privacy
Manage school website + online presence
otherDuties: Support school events requiring IT setup (online meetings, presentations)
Train staff + students on new software / systems
minimumQualifications: Associate or Bachelor's in IT / Computer Science
1-3 years IT support / system admin experience
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet single-column value 21. Equivalent.pdf reclassifies as non-teaching support (no triplet) and tags as Classified tier.

positions.title: Head of Laboratory
positions.description: Oversees the operation and maintenance of school laboratory facilities. Ensures a safe and conducive learning environment and promotes scientific inquiry.
majorDuties: Laboratory management — plan, organize, manage operations (scheduling, budgeting, resources)
Safety supervision — compliance with chemical, equipment, biological safety regulations
Equipment maintenance — repair, calibration
Inventory management — supplies, equipment records, ordering
Staff supervision — manage and train lab technicians
Student supervision — supervise lab activities; safety + procedure understanding
otherDuties: Collaborate with teachers on curriculum-aligned lab activities
Assist faculty research projects with technical support
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree in science or related field
Strong knowledge of lab safety procedures
trainingDevelopment: Workshops, conferences, online courses on lab practices + technologies
positions.teachingHoursNote: Source-sheet value. Low number suggests primarily technical, not instructional role — methodology pending.

positions.title: Laboratory Technician
positions.description: Performs tests + experiments (clinical / research / environmental) to assist scientists, teachers, students. Maintains lab equipment.
majorDuties: Prepare samples + conduct experiments following established protocols
Maintain lab equipment
Record + analyse experiment data
Follow safety protocols + cleanliness
Assist in developing new techniques + processes
otherDuties: Train new staff / interns in lab procedures
minimumQualifications: Associate or Bachelor's degree in relevant field (Biology, Chemistry); D2 for technician; D1 for laborant
trainingDevelopment: Health and Safety in the Science Laboratory
ICT tools for inventory and lab reporting
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet single-column value 4 (low — implies primarily technical). Equivalent.pdf 'Laborant' row reclassifies as non-teaching specialist (no triplet) and tags as Certificated tier.

positions.title: Librarian
positions.description: Manages the library's collection of books, periodicals, educational materials and assists students in research + use of resources.
majorDuties: Manage library collection (select, order, catalogue, shelve)
Produce monthly book-collection update report
Develop + maintain library policies (circulation, overdue, hours)
Operate library 08:15-18:00 with 11:15-12:00 break
Record borrowing + returns
Weekly late-fee income report
Assist students + faculty (research, recommendations)
Serve visitors on book consultation
Plan library programmes (book clubs, reading challenges, author visits)
minimumQualifications: Bachelor's degree in Library Science or related field
positions.teachingHoursNote: Original source-sheet single-column value 4. Equivalent.pdf reclassifies as non-teaching specialist (no triplet) and tags as Certificated tier.

positions.title: Foundation Representative (Yayasan)
positions.description: Strategic governance role above the Principal. Owns the partner school's foundation/board interface with Eduversal HQ. In the Phase-2 Principal Evaluation Module, Foundation Representatives are the primary appraisers of school principals. Eduversal-specific: maps to AH sub-role `foundation_representative`. Foundation Reps are EXCLUDED from principal coaching sessions (confidentiality, Charter NN1 spirit).
majorDuties: Strategic governance of the partner school within the foundation
Appraise the Principal annually (Principal Appraisal v1, F1-F5 + F_LEAD framework)
Conduct principal observation visits (8-foci rubric, E/D/N coding)
Approve school strategic plans + budget at foundation level
Interface with Eduversal HQ on network-wide initiatives
otherDuties: Mentor Principals on long-term leadership development
minimumQualifications: Senior leadership background (typically former Principal or senior educator)
Familiarity with Cambridge School Leader Standards
positions.certificationTierNote: Equivalent.pdf carries the Foundation Representative row but all four numeric columns are blank, consistent with a governance-only (non-instructional, non-graded) role.
positions.source: gap_added:2026-05-13 (missing from source sheet; required by Phase-2 Principal Evaluation Module) + equivalent-pdf:2026-05-14

positions.title: Academic Coordinator
positions.description: Cross-subject academic operations: timetabling, assessment scheduling, curriculum coherence across subjects. Differs from Subject Leader (single-subject ownership) by spanning the full subject portfolio at the grade level. Eduversal-specific: maps to AH sub-role `academic_coordinator`.
majorDuties: Cross-subject curriculum coherence
Network-wide timetabling + assessment scheduling
Manage assessment windows (chapter tests, EASE) at school level
Coordinate subject leaders' work to prevent overlap + gaps
Liaise with Cambridge Coordinator on programme delivery
positions.certificationTierNote: Equivalent.pdf row 'Academic Dept Coordinator' is treated as this catalogue's Academic Coordinator. No teaching-hour triplet in source — consistent with a network-wide cross-subject coordination role.

positions.title: Cambridge Coordinator
positions.description: Owns the school's Cambridge programme delivery: IGCSE, AS-A-Level, Primary + Lower Secondary Checkpoint. Liaison with Cambridge International + Cambridge International registration office. Eduversal-specific: maps to AH sub-role `cambridge_coordinator`. Critical role — Cambridge curriculum is Eduversal's core operation.
majorDuties: Cambridge programme registration + entries management
Liaise with Cambridge International (CAIE)
Manage Cambridge exam logistics + invigilation rosters
Cambridge syllabus + scheme-of-work alignment per subject
Cambridge Checkpoint (Primary + Lower Secondary) coordination
IGCSE + AS-A-Level entry decisions per student
otherDuties: Cambridge results analysis + reporting to principal + foundation
Cambridge professional development coordination across teachers
minimumQualifications: Familiarity with Cambridge syllabus codes (0580/0610/0620/0625/9709/9700/9701/9702 etc.)
positions.certificationTierNote: Equivalent.pdf row 'Cambridge Exam Officer' (24/4/20, Certificated) is treated as this catalogue's Cambridge Coordinator. Exam-officer is the narrower CAIE-facing slice of the broader Cambridge Coordinator brief.
positions.source: gap_added:2026-05-13 (in CLAUDE.md sub-role list but missing from source sheet) + equivalent-pdf:2026-05-14

positions.title: Induction Mentor
positions.description: Year-1 induction mentor for newly-hired staff. Charter NN3 BINDING: must hold active `mentor_certifications/{uid}_mentor_base` document BEFORE assignment to a mentee. Charter NN2 BINDING: never reads named journal entries from mentee. Maps to TH sub-role `subject_leader` (subject mentor) or one of the principal-coaching director roles (leadership mentor).
majorDuties: Mentor a Year-1 mentee through the induction programme (3 role-specific handbooks: subject_teacher, school_principal, hq_subject_specialist)
Conduct monthly 4-domain observation (10-walkthrough cycle)
Glow / Grow / Go feedback per observation
Maintain mentee journal NN2 boundary (HQ never reads named entries)
Mood-pulse review + escalation per Significant Concern Policy
minimumQualifications: Subject mastery (for subject-teacher mentees) OR leadership credentials (for principal mentees)
trainingDevelopment: Cambridge Mentoring Guide 2020 (8-module viewer in TH /mentor-certification)
positions.source: gap_added:2026-05-13 (missing from source sheet; required by NN3 binding charter)

positions.title: HQ Director (Primary Schools / Secondary Schools)
positions.description: Eduversal HQ senior leadership. Two streams: Primary Schools Director + Secondary Schools Director. Network-wide oversight; sits above HQ Subject Specialists. Bypasses subject-specialty + page-access gates (mirrors central_admin). In Phase-2 Principal Evaluation Module, HQ Directors are the mentors for school principals (coaching sessions, year-1 + year-2+ rubric foci). Eduversal-specific: maps to CH sub-role `director`.
majorDuties: Strategic leadership across the partner-school network
Mentor school principals through coaching sessions (CH `/principal-coaching-session`, 5-stage 60-min agenda)
Approve network-wide initiatives + curriculum decisions
Manage HQ Subject Specialist team
Network performance review
positions.source: gap_added:2026-05-13 (in CLAUDE.md sub-role list but missing from source sheet)

positions.title: HQ Subject Specialist
positions.description: HQ-side subject expert authoring chapter tests + EASE items + curriculum coherence for one or more subjects. Filtered network-wide by `ch_subjects[]` field. Specialty values: math, biology, chemistry, physics, science (combined-science for Checkpoint), english, bahasa, religion. Charter NN1: Specialist appraisal visits to partner schools are 1-2 per year. Eduversal-specific: maps to CH sub-role `coordinator` (a coordinator IS a subject specialist; always filtered by ch_subjects[]).
majorDuties: Author chapter tests via CH `/chapter-test-author` for assigned subjects
Author EASE items via CH `/ease-item-author` (3-band: easy/medium/hard)
Curriculum coherence + scheme-of-work review across subjects
Subject Specialist appraisal visits to partner schools (1-2 per year)
Mentor school subject leaders network-wide
Cambridge syllabus alignment + Indonesian KM ↔ Cambridge cross-mapping
otherDuties: Maintain the subject pacing + scheme-of-work seeds
minimumQualifications: 8+ years subject teaching including Cambridge programme experience
trainingDevelopment: Cambridge Teacher Standards 2023 (Specialist track — 9/27 CTS hybrid)
Item-writing certification (for chapter tests + EASE items)
Specialist Content Framework (hybrid coaching-observer + subject-deepening + network strategy)
positions.source: gap_added:2026-05-13 (in CLAUDE.md but missing from source sheet)

positions.title: HQ Coordinator (Cross-Functional)
positions.description: Cross-functional HQ operations: assessment management, project management, network-wide initiatives. Distinct from HQ Subject Specialist (which is also CH `coordinator` sub-role) — this is a coordinator without a subject specialty (`ch_subjects[]` empty intentionally, but the user is configured for cross-functional work).
positions.gapFlag: Ambiguity: CH sub-role `coordinator` is both 'subject specialist' (when ch_subjects[] populated) AND 'cross-functional coordinator' (when not). HR + tech to agree on whether to introduce a sub-role flag distinguishing these.
majorDuties: Cross-functional HQ operations + project management
Support HQ Director on network-wide initiatives
Manage assessment delivery operations (chapter tests, EASE windows)
Coordinate HQ-side mail composer campaigns

positions.title: HQ IT Coordinator
positions.description: Network-wide IT operations: Firestore + Firebase backend, Vercel deployment, network infrastructure across CH/AH/TH/SH/RH. Different from school IT Staff (school-bounded).
majorDuties: Network-wide IT infrastructure (Firebase, Vercel, GitHub, mail-service)
Coordinate school IT staff across the network
Manage CH-AH-TH-SH-RH platform deployments
DPO advisory role (UU PDP 2024 compliance)
positions.certificationTierNote: Equivalent.pdf carries 'IT Dept Coordinator' as the school-side analogue (AM&C tier). This catalogue keeps both the school IT Dept Coordinator brief and the HQ IT Coordinator brief on one record — same tier applies.

positions.title: Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL)
positions.description: Child Protection / Safeguarding lead. Required by modern international school accreditation standards (incl. Cambridge accreditation). Single accountable officer for child protection cases at each school. Coordinates with School Psychologist + School Nurse + Guidance Coordinator on case escalation.
positions.gapFlag: Missing from source sheet despite being required by Cambridge accreditation. Mandatory under modern international-school standards. HR must appoint at every partner school.
majorDuties: Single accountable officer for child protection at the school
Maintain child protection register + case files
Coordinate case escalation (psychologist, nurse, external authorities)
Train all staff in child protection awareness (annual)
Liaise with Indonesian authorities (KPAI, social services)
minimumQualifications: Senior teacher / counsellor / psychologist with 5+ years experience
Recognised safeguarding qualification (e.g. Cambridge / NSPCC equivalent)
Familiarity with Indonesian child protection law (UU 35/2014)

positions.title: Data Protection Officer (DPO)
positions.description: UU PDP 2024 (Personal Data Protection Law) compliance officer. Required given Eduversal stores PII for students/{uid}, parent_share_tokens, careers applications. Often shared role with HQ IT Coordinator or Legal Officer for smaller schools — but the accountability MUST be assigned.
positions.gapFlag: Missing from source sheet. UU PDP 2024 in force since 2024. Pending Eduversal legal review — may be combined with Legal Officer or HQ IT Coordinator role.
majorDuties: UU PDP 2024 compliance assessment + audit
Data subject request handling (access, correction, deletion)
Maintain personal-data inventory across CH/AH/TH/SH/RH
Breach notification procedure
Data Processor Agreements (DPAs) with vendors (Firebase, Resend, Railway, Latihan.id)
otherDuties: Privacy impact assessments for new features
minimumQualifications: Familiarity with Firebase + cloud data flows

positions.title: SEN / EAL Coordinator
positions.description: Special Educational Needs (SEN) + English as Additional Language (EAL) coordination. Critical in Cambridge English-medium schools where students are non-native English speakers and / or have learning differences. Coordinates Individualized Education Plans (IEPs) with School Psychologist + Subject Teachers.
positions.gapFlag: Missing from source sheet. Common standard in international schools — especially relevant for Eduversal as a Cambridge-curriculum English-medium provider in Indonesia.
majorDuties: Identify SEN + EAL students (admission + ongoing)
Develop IEPs in collaboration with school psychologist + subject teachers
EAL pull-out / push-in support sessions
Differentiation strategy coaching for classroom teachers
Liaise with external SEN specialists when needed
otherDuties: Coordinate Cambridge access arrangements for SEN students at exam time
minimumQualifications: SEN qualification (e.g. NASCO, IB SEN, or equivalent)

positions.title: Hiring Manager
positions.description: Hiring decision authority for teacher recruitment. Maps to TH sub-role `hiring_manager`. In the Careers Module flow: receives candidate applications, conducts hiring-funnel reviews via TH `/careers-admin` + `/careers-compare`. Bound to own school (rule: `isHiringMgrSameSchool()`).
majorDuties: Review applications for open positions at own school
Compose interview panel + assign interviewers
Approve offer / reject decisions in /careers-admin
Coordinate with HR on contract logistics
otherDuties: Liaise with Eduversal HQ on network-wide hiring guidance
minimumQualifications: Senior teaching staff / VP / Principal status

positions.title: Interviewer
positions.description: Teacher-recruitment interviewer. Maps to TH sub-role `interviewer`. Conducts structured interviews + scores via `/interview-scorecard`. Submitted scorecards are immutable at the rule level.
majorDuties: Conduct structured candidate interviews on assigned applications
Score interviews via TH `/interview-scorecard` (4-category × 18-question framework)
Submit immutable scorecard at end of interview
otherDuties: Provide qualitative feedback notes on candidates
minimumQualifications: Trained on the network interview rubric

positions.title: Head of Department
positions.description: National-mandated department head role: leads a single curricular department across the network of grade levels at the school. Distinct from Subject Leader (which is a Cambridge-track single-subject ownership inside a school). Sits between Subject Leader and Vice Principal in the org-chart. Carries the heaviest non-teaching deduction in the PDF set (24/12/12).
majorDuties: Lead the department across grade levels at the school
Department-wide curriculum coherence and assessment alignment
Mentor Subject Leaders within the department
Department budget + resource planning
Liaise with HQ Subject Specialists on subject-coherence
minimumQualifications: 5+ years subject teaching + 2+ years subject leadership
positions.source: equivalent-pdf:2026-05-14 (missing from original source sheet; required at every Indonesian partner school)

positions.title: School Culture Coordinator
positions.description: Coordinates school-wide culture, values, and community identity programmes. Eduversal-specific note: this role is sometimes folded into Guidance Coordinator at smaller schools; Equivalent.pdf treats it as a separate line with 24/4/20.
majorDuties: Plan school-wide culture and values programmes
Coordinate assemblies + culture week + tradition events
Author + maintain school culture handbook
otherDuties: Liaise with Parent Coordinator + OSIS on cross-cutting culture themes

positions.title: Olympiad Coordinator
positions.description: Co-curricular role: identifies, prepares, and accompanies high-performing students for national and international academic olympiads (OSN, IMO, IPhO, IBO, etc.). Closes one of the gaps flagged in SI-13 (athletic / music / arts / olympiad co-curricular coordinators absent from original source).
majorDuties: Identify olympiad-eligible students per subject
Schedule + supervise olympiad training rounds
Manage school + national + international olympiad entries
Coordinate Olympiad Trainers across subjects
otherDuties: Liaise with HQ Subject Specialists for advanced training resources
minimumQualifications: Prior olympiad participation / coaching experience preferred

positions.title: Olympiad Trainer
positions.description: Subject-specific olympiad trainer. Reports to Olympiad Coordinator. May be in-house teacher or external coach. PDF row carries no teaching-hour triplet — implies an hours-by-engagement structure rather than a contracted base load.
majorDuties: Train olympiad-eligible students in assigned subject
Run training rounds + mock olympiad problem sets
otherDuties: Report training progress to Olympiad Coordinator

positions.title: Club Coordinator
positions.description: Manages the school's student-club portfolio (sports, arts, debate, language, hobby clubs). Coordinates ECA scheduling, club budgets, and club-leader development. Closes part of SI-13 (co-curricular coordinator gap).
majorDuties: Maintain school club register + termly schedule
Coordinate club-leader teacher mentors
Manage shared ECA resources + venues
otherDuties: Liaise with OSIS Coordinator on student-led club activities

positions.title: Competition Coordinator
positions.description: Coordinates external academic competitions outside the olympiad track (e.g. essay contests, debate tournaments, maths fairs). Distinct from Olympiad Coordinator (which focuses on national/international olympiad pipeline only).
majorDuties: Identify + register students for external competitions
Coordinate competition prep + travel
Maintain competition results archive
otherDuties: Liaise with Public Relations on competition coverage

positions.title: Science Project Coordinator
positions.description: Coordinates the school's science fair / science project programme. Overlaps with Project Coordinator but specialised to science discipline (biology, chemistry, physics, science). Often paired with Head of Laboratory.
majorDuties: Plan + run the school science fair
Coach student science-project teams
Manage equipment + materials budget for projects
otherDuties: Liaise with HQ Subject Specialists for project ideas + judging

positions.title: English Club & Owlypia Coordinator
positions.description: Network-specific co-curricular role: runs the school English Club and represents the school at the Owlypia international academic competition. Combined into a single line in the source PDF.
majorDuties: Run weekly English Club sessions
Manage Owlypia school registration + team training + travel
Lead English-language enrichment events
otherDuties: Coordinate with EAL coordinator on English-development pathways

positions.title: EMC Coordinator
positions.description: Eduversal Music Competition (EMC) coordinator. Per-school music coordinator for the network-wide EMC programme. HR confirmation pending on full programme spec.
positions.gapFlag: Acronym 'EMC' inferred to be Eduversal Music Competition based on the network's co-curricular pattern. HR confirmation pending.
majorDuties: Coordinate EMC participation at school level
Run EMC training rounds

positions.title: OSEBI Volunteer & Coordinator
positions.description: OSEBI volunteer programme coordinator. OSEBI is an Indonesian language-and-culture extracurricular network. HR confirmation pending on full programme spec.
positions.gapFlag: Acronym 'OSEBI' inferred from context (Indonesian extracurricular network). HR confirmation pending.
majorDuties: Coordinate OSEBI participation at school level
Mentor OSEBI student volunteers

positions.title: Administration (Generic Admin Staff)
positions.description: Generic administration role row from Equivalent.pdf (no tier specified). Used as a placeholder until HR clarifies which specific admin position this row maps to — likely a junior administrative assistant tier below Secretary / School Admin.
positions.gapFlag: Source PDF row simply labelled 'Administration' with no further description, tier, or hours. HR clarification needed: junior admin assistant? Admin pool? Or duplicate of School Admin?
majorDuties: General admin support across the school office

positions.title: System Admin
positions.description: Network / system administrator. Distinct from IT Staff (front-line support) — runs the school's backend systems, user accounts, infrastructure. Sits one rung below the HQ IT Coordinator.
majorDuties: School-side system administration
User account + permission management
Backend infrastructure operations
Backup + recovery procedures

positions.title: Admission Officer
positions.description: Manages the school admissions cycle: enquiries, application processing, entrance assessments, enrolment confirmations. Front-line interface for prospective parents.
majorDuties: Manage admissions inquiry pipeline
Process applications + entrance assessments
Coordinate with Public Relations on intake campaigns
Maintain admissions database
otherDuties: Run school tours for prospective parents

positions.title: Facility Manager
positions.description: Manages school facilities: buildings, grounds, equipment, maintenance contractors, safety inspections. Listed under Facilities category in source PDF.
majorDuties: Facility operations + preventive maintenance
Manage facility contractors
Coordinate safety inspections + drills
Manage facility budget
otherDuties: Coordinate with Designated Safeguarding Lead on physical safety

positions.title: Accountant Coordinator
positions.description: Department-level accounting coordinator. Supervises the school accountant team and reports to Foundation Representative on financial controls. Tier: Administrative, Management & Confidential.
majorDuties: Supervise accounting team + reporting
Financial controls + audit prep
Coordinate with HQ Finance on network reporting

positions.title: Alumni Dept Coordinator
positions.description: Department-level alumni coordinator. Distinct from the per-school Alumni Coordinator (which is teaching-track 24/8/16, Classified) — this is the cross-network alumni programme coordinator on AM&C tier.
majorDuties: Network-wide alumni programme coordination
Alumni database + CRM management
Coordinate alumni newsletters + reunions
otherDuties: Liaise with HQ on alumni-pipeline metrics

positions.title: College Counselling Coordinator
positions.description: Department-level coordinator above per-school College Counsellor. Owns the network-wide career-planning syllabus + university partnerships.
majorDuties: Network-wide career planning syllabus
University partnership + MoU management
Coordinate per-school College Counsellor team

positions.title: Dialog Coordinator
positions.description: Network-internal communication-and-dialog coordinator. HR confirmation pending — likely an internal stakeholder-engagement / change-communication role. AM&C tier per PDF.
positions.gapFlag: Role name 'Dialog Coordinator' is ambiguous in source. HR clarification needed on scope (stakeholder dialog? Inter-school dialog? Crisis communication?).
majorDuties: Internal-stakeholder dialog management

positions.title: Digital Marketing Coordinator
positions.description: Network-level digital marketing coordinator. Sits above the per-school Digital Marketing Specialist. Owns network-wide brand + acquisition campaigns.
majorDuties: Network-wide digital marketing strategy
Brand consistency across schools
Lead generation + enrolment campaigns at network level

positions.title: Guidance Dept Coordinator
positions.description: Department-level coordinator above per-school Guidance Coordinator. Network-wide guidance + pastoral programme.
majorDuties: Network-wide guidance programme strategy
Coordinate per-school Guidance Coordinators
Mental-health + safeguarding programme oversight at network level

positions.title: IT Dept Coordinator (School-side)
positions.description: School-level IT department coordinator. Sits above IT Staff at one school; reports up to HQ IT Coordinator at network level. AM&C tier per PDF. Distinct from `it_coordinator_hq` which is the network-wide role.
majorDuties: Coordinate school IT team
Coordinate with HQ IT on deployments
Manage school IT budget
minimumQualifications: 5+ years sysadmin or IT team-lead experience

positions.title: Mothers Club Coordinator
positions.description: Coordinates the school Mothers' Club — a parent-engagement programme common to Indonesian private schools. AM&C tier.
majorDuties: Run Mothers Club programmes + events
Liaise between mothers' community and school administration
Coordinate Mothers Club volunteers

positions.title: Parent Dept Coordinator
positions.description: Department-level parent coordinator. Sits above per-school Parent Coordinator. Network-wide parent-engagement programme. AM&C tier.
majorDuties: Network-wide parent-engagement strategy
Coordinate per-school Parent Coordinators
Parent-survey network rollup

positions.title: Extra Curricular Coordinator
positions.description: Cross-cutting ECA coordinator. Sits above Club / Competition / Olympiad / Science Project / English Club / EMC / OSEBI coordinators. No teaching-hour triplet in source PDF.
majorDuties: Cross-portfolio ECA strategy
Coordinate all co-curricular sub-coordinators
Maintain ECA calendar + budget at school level
otherDuties: Liaise with HQ on ECA network metrics

positions.title: Community Engagement 1
positions.description: Community-engagement role (Tier 1). Source PDF uses a non-24 base load (Optimum 20 / Equivalent 8 / Suggested 12) — anomalous vs all other rows which use Optimum 24. HR clarification pending on what 'Tier 1' vs 'Tier 2' means.
positions.gapFlag: Equivalent.pdf rows 'Community Engagement 1' and 'Community Engagement 2' use Optimum = 20 (not 24). HR confirmation needed on whether this is a different base load (e.g. part-time contract) or a source-sheet error.
majorDuties: Run community-engagement programmes
Liaise with local community partners

positions.title: Community Engagement 2
positions.description: Community-engagement role (Tier 2). Source PDF: Optimum 20 / Equivalent 8 / Suggested (blank). Same non-24 base anomaly as Community Engagement 1.
positions.gapFlag: Optimum = 20 (not 24); Suggested column blank in source. HR clarification pending.
majorDuties: Senior community-engagement programmes
Mentor Community Engagement 1 staff
positions.teachingHoursModelNote: Non-standard base (Optimum 20) AND Suggested column blank in source. Flagged for HR clarification.

positions.title: Homeroom Teacher SD
positions.scopeLabel: Out of scope — SD (Grade 1-6) coverage deferred (see SI-06).
positions.description: Primary-school (SD) homeroom teacher. Listed in Equivalent.pdf but all four numeric columns are blank. Out of scope for the current Y7-8 pilot.
positions.gapFlag: SD (primary school) is out of scope for the current Eduversal SMP/SMA catalogue. Row preserved for K-12 expansion review (see SI-06).

positions.title: Principal KG (Kindergarten Principal)
positions.description: Division principal — leads the instructional line within KG. Reports directly to FR/GM. Coordinates with the division Guidance Counselor on dual matters but does not supervise the guidance line.
positions.notes: KG division principal — added per School Organizational & Meeting Framework v1.0 (2026).

positions.title: Principal SD (Primary School Principal)
positions.description: Division principal — leads the instructional line within SD. Reports directly to FR/GM. Coordinates with the division Guidance Counselor on dual matters but does not supervise the guidance line.
positions.notes: SD division principal — added per School Organizational & Meeting Framework v1.0 (2026).

positions.title: Guidance Counselor KG
positions.description: Division guidance counselor — leads the welfare & counselling line within KG. Reports directly to FR/GM at the same hierarchical level as the division principal. Per Framework v1.0 NN1, neither line supervises the other.
positions.notes: KG division guidance counselor — added per Framework v1.0 (2026). Parallel to principal_kg.

positions.title: Guidance Counselor SD
positions.description: Division guidance counselor — leads the welfare & counselling line within SD. Reports directly to FR/GM at the same hierarchical level as the division principal. Per Framework v1.0 NN1, neither line supervises the other.
positions.notes: SD division guidance counselor — added per Framework v1.0 (2026). Parallel to principal_sd.

positions.title: Guidance Counselor SMP
positions.description: Division guidance counselor — leads the welfare & counselling line within SMP. Reports directly to FR/GM at the same hierarchical level as the division principal. Per Framework v1.0 NN1, neither line supervises the other.
positions.notes: SMP division guidance counselor — added per Framework v1.0 (2026). Parallel to principal_smp.

positions.title: Guidance Counselor SMA
positions.description: Division guidance counselor — leads the welfare & counselling line within SMA. Reports directly to FR/GM at the same hierarchical level as the division principal. Per Framework v1.0 NN1, neither line supervises the other.
positions.notes: SMA division guidance counselor — added per Framework v1.0 (2026). Parallel to principal_sma.

openSourceIssues.title: Principal SMP / Vice Principal SMP profiles incomplete
openSourceIssues.description: Source rows near-empty. Critical since the 2026-05-10 Eduversal pilot is Y7-8 (SMP). HR fill provided below the gap-flag but needs validation.

openSourceIssues.title: HQ-side roles missing from source
openSourceIssues.description: Source covers only school-side staff. HQ Director, HQ Subject Specialist (with ch_subjects[]), HQ Coordinator, HQ IT Coordinator added by HR fill (codes 7.1-7.4).

openSourceIssues.title: Cambridge Coordinator, Foundation Representative, Academic Coordinator not in source
openSourceIssues.description: All present in CLAUDE.md sub-role lists; added by HR fill (codes 0.1-0.3). Foundation Rep is the Principal appraiser in Phase-2.

openSourceIssues.title: Induction Mentor profile missing
openSourceIssues.description: NN3 binding charter requires mentor_certifications/{uid}_mentor_base before assignment. Added by HR fill (code 0.4).

openSourceIssues.title: DSL (Designated Safeguarding Lead) + DPO (Data Protection Officer) not in source
openSourceIssues.description: DSL is a Cambridge accreditation requirement. DPO is UU PDP 2024 statutory. Both added by HR fill (codes 8.1-8.2).

openSourceIssues.title: SD / TK / Early Years not in scope
openSourceIssues.description: Document covers SMP + SMA only. K-12 expansion (SD Principal, TK Principal, Early Years Coordinator) deferred — flag at scope review.
openSourceIssues.resolution_notes: KG + SD divisions formally added to scope per School Organizational & Meeting Framework v1.0 (2026) §3 — divisional symmetry rule mandates KG/Primary/Middle/High replication. 6 new positions added to JSON: principal_kg, principal_sd, guidance_kg, guidance_sd, guidance_smp, guidance_sma (parallel dual-line per Framework §2.1 NN1).

openSourceIssues.title: Teaching-Hours Equivalent methodology not published
openSourceIssues.description: Values 4-21 inherited from source sheet without rationale. Methodology key + formula MUST be published before salary / contract use. See advisoryDisclaimers.teaching_hours_equivalent.

openSourceIssues.title: MBTI mis-applied as hiring signal
openSourceIssues.description: MBTI test-retest reliability is known-low. Risk of discrimination claim if used in hiring. See advisoryDisclaimers.mbti — preserve as reference-only; Big Five recommended for any future personality instrument.

openSourceIssues.title: Reporting lines / org chart missing in source
openSourceIssues.description: Each position now has reportsTo[] + directReports[] + appraisedBy[]. Validate end-to-end consistency before publishing.
openSourceIssues.resolution_notes: Authoritative reports-to topology now defined by School Organizational & Meeting Framework v1.0 (2026) §3-4. 20-row mapping table is the canonical reference. Eduversal Foundation v1.0 mandates this topology as non-negotiable for partner schools. Org chart rendered against canonical_sources.framework-v1.0-2026.

openSourceIssues.title: Promotion criteria not specified
openSourceIssues.description: careerPathway[] lists next-rung roles but does NOT specify years-of-experience, KPI thresholds, or competency-level prerequisites. Recommended: layer in `promotionCriteria` per pathway link in v1.1.

openSourceIssues.title: Cross-system mapping incomplete
openSourceIssues.description: ratingSystems[] field added per role. Validate the matrix Teacher/SubjectLeader/Principal/Specialist × KPI/Appraisal/Competency before publishing.

openSourceIssues.title: Operational staff (maintenance, security, cleaning, kitchen) not covered
openSourceIssues.description: Likely outsourced. Flag explicitly in v1.1 to confirm scope.

openSourceIssues.title: Athletic / Music / Arts coordinators absent
openSourceIssues.description: Co-curricular coverage limited to OSIS Coordinator + Project Coordinator. Specialist co-curricular coordinator roles to add.

openSourceIssues.title: AFT mentee profile missing
openSourceIssues.description: AFT Coordinator covers the supervisor side. Pre-service teacher (AFT mentee) is a temporary status not currently captured.

openSourceIssues.title: Salary band / pay scale information absent
openSourceIssues.description: Document is a position catalogue not a pay-grade reference. P1-P5 band mapping deferred to a future HR-Ops layer.

openSourceIssues.title: Source-sheet duplicates merged + renumbered

openSourceIssues.title: Equivalent.pdf reconciled (2026-05-14) — 24/Equivalent/Suggested triplet replaces single-column 4-21 value
openSourceIssues.description: Career Growth Equivalent Hours sheet (Equivalent.pdf) imported on 2026-05-14. Adds a 3-column teaching-hours triplet (Optimum 24 baseline / Equivalent hours deducted as allowance / Suggested = actual teaching hours), a certification-tier flag (Certificated / Classified / AM&C), and per-row sheet notes. 13 net-new positions added (codes 10.x co-curricular, 11.x admin, 12.x dept-coordinator, 13.x community-engagement+SD). Original single-column 'teachingHoursEquivalent' values 4-21 preserved on each row for back-compat but the new triplet model takes precedence. Open gaps: 'Community Engagement 1/2' use non-24 base (Optimum 20); EMC + OSEBI acronyms inferred from context; 'Dialog Coordinator' scope ambiguous; 'Administration' (generic row) vs 'School Admin' overlap unresolved.

openSourceIssues.title: Per-school vs department-coordinator duplication
openSourceIssues.description: Equivalent.pdf lists both per-school coordinators (e.g. Alumni Coordinator 24/8/16, Classified) and parallel department-level coordinators with no teaching-hour triplet (Alumni Dept Coordinator, AM&C). Catalogue keeps both because HR confirms they are distinct seniority tiers. Validate the network-vs-school report-line for each parallel pair before publishing v1.2.
